formulas.name: IPAT Equation
formulas.variables: I = Impact on the environment, P = Population, A = Affluence, T = Technology
formulas.notes: The IPAT equation helps in understanding the impact of human activities on the environment.

formulas.name: Cradle to Grave Approach
formulas.notes: The cradle to grave approach emphasizes the entire lifecycle of a product, from production to disposal.

formulas.name: 3Rs (Reduce, Reuse, Recycle)
formulas.notes: The 3Rs are strategies for reducing waste and conserving natural resources.

formulas.name: Carrying Capacity
formulas.notes: Carrying capacity refers to the maximum number of individuals that an environment can support.

formulas.name: Eco-Friendly
formulas.notes: Eco-friendly practices and products minimize harm to the environment.

formulas.name: Sustainable Development
formulas.notes: Sustainable development balances economic, social, and environmental needs.

formulas.name: Polluter Pays Principle
formulas.notes: The polluter pays principle states that those responsible for environmental pollution should bear the costs of cleanup and restoration.
